summary: Excellent soundboard transfer of a quirky 1981 club show – acoustic gems like “Cassidy” and “Ripple” plus a fiery electric set highlighted by “Birdsong” and “Lovelight”.
review: The Charlie Miller‑sourced soundboard capture is clear and well‑balanced, preserving Brent’s clean keys and the band’s on‑stage spontaneity.  While the acoustic half feels a bit uneven for some listeners, the electric portion delivers a high‑energy, almost Vegas‑style set with standout moments such as “Birdsong”, “Lovelight” and a scorching “Samson & Delilah”.  Overall the performance is celebrated by reviewers despite a few song choices they’d skip.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high star ratings (mostly 4‑5) and detailed praise for both the music and the transfer indicate a strong show with an excellent soundboard recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Transfer by Charlie Miller praised as “great”, clean mix, Brent’s keyboards sit nicely, no major drop‑outs or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic set is a pleasant, if not peak‑year, collection of bouncy tunes; the electric half feels like an hour‑long novelty/club gig that erupts into high‑octane jams, especially after the Lovelight transition.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Playing on rented gear (Yamaha SC1200/Strat) – described as “on fire”, shredding, though one reviewer notes a thinner tone due to missing stereo splitter.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lots of praise – “tears it up” on Birdsong, high in the mix and delivers great moments throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Ready to sing; his vocals noted positively in the energetic second set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s clean keyboard tones highlighted as a highlight of the night.
